relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Abraham journeyed again as a sojourner, moving through the Negev into Gerar.
Fear repeated an old pattern: Sarah was called sister and taken into another king’s house.
The Divine entered Abimelech’s dream and warned him before the harm became complete.
Abimelech pleaded innocence, asking whether a righteous nation would be killed.
He appealed to integrity of heart and clean hands.
The Divine acknowledged his integrity and revealed that restraint itself had been protection.
Sarah had to be returned, and Abraham’s prophetic role would become intercession.
Abimelech rose early and brought the warning into the open, and fear moved through his house.
He confronted Abraham with the weight of what had nearly happened.
He asked what Abraham had seen that made him act this way.
Abraham named his fear: he assumed there was no fear of the Divine in that place.
He explained the partial truth beneath the deception.
The sister claim had become a wandering survival strategy from the time he left his father’s house.
Abimelech returned Sarah and gave wealth, reversing the taking.
He opened the land before Abraham instead of retaliating.
Sarah was publicly covered and vindicated after being placed in danger.
Abraham prayed, and healing came to Abimelech’s house.
The closed wombs of the house were opened after Sarah was restored.
